text.content:
Since 1903, all tires, tubes, valves, and rims have followed standards established by the Tire & Rim Association of America (T&RA). Such a move benefits manufacturers, farmers, and growers in the way they source tires and fit their equipment.

The European market would also follow this model, with the European Tyre and Rim Technical Organization’s (ERTRO) creation in the 1960s. ERTRO standardized pneumatic tires and rims in the same way as the T&RA, providing a benchmark for the industries to follow.

Each size that manufacturers develop must fall within the following tolerance for any given tire size in millimeters or inches:

Each size that manufacturers develop must fall within the following tolerance for any given tire size in millimeters or inches:

text.content: Tire Overall Diameter (O.D.) = + / - 2% versus the Industry Size Standard

rich_text.plain_text: Tire Overall Diameter (O.D.) = + / - 2% versus the Industry Size Standard

text.content: Tire Section Width (S.W.) = + / - 4% versus the Industry Size Standard

rich_text.plain_text: Tire Section Width (S.W.) = + / - 4% versus the Industry Size Standard

text.content: In addition to the above design limits, there are other considerations that affect tire design and construction. For example, tire dimensions can change simply from the tire’s type and construction whether standard, IF, or VF. Other design challenges and considerations include:

rich_text.plain_text: In addition to the above design limits, there are other considerations that affect tire design and construction. For example, tire dimensions can change simply from the tire’s type and construction whether standard, IF, or VF. Other design challenges and considerations include:

text.content: Tread Depth

rich_text.plain_text: Tread Depth

text.content: The tire tread depth based on the lug design affects the overall diameter (OD) of a standard R-1 pattern versus the same sized tire with deeper R-1W, or R-2 tread depth.

rich_text.plain_text: The tire tread depth based on the lug design affects the overall diameter (OD) of a standard R-1 pattern versus the same sized tire with deeper R-1W, or R-2 tread depth.

text.content: Tire tread depth will also differ from manufacturer to manufacturer which will affect the tire geometry despite tires being of the same size and within the above industry tolerances.

rich_text.plain_text: Tire tread depth will also differ from manufacturer to manufacturer which will affect the tire geometry despite tires being of the same size and within the above industry tolerances.

text.content: Tire Weight

rich_text.plain_text: Tire Weight

text.content: In addition, tire weight is not mandated in the standard beyond the above dimensional requirements, therefore the type of tire or materials used will affect the deflection and geometry of the tire under load.

rich_text.plain_text: In addition, tire weight is not mandated in the standard beyond the above dimensional requirements, therefore the type of tire or materials used will affect the deflection and geometry of the tire under load.

text.content: Tire Inflation

rich_text.plain_text: Tire Inflation

text.content: Specifically, in bias ply tires, the different ply rating or load ratings of the same size can result in different inflated overall diameter (OD) based on a given air pressure/tire load.

rich_text.plain_text: Specifically, in bias ply tires, the different ply rating or load ratings of the same size can result in different inflated overall diameter (OD) based on a given air pressure/tire load.

text.content: Radial tires with a steel belt package or belts may have different inflated OD from the same size tire with nylon breakers.

rich_text.plain_text: Radial tires with a steel belt package or belts may have different inflated OD from the same size tire with nylon breakers.

text.content: Construction and Design

rich_text.plain_text: Construction and Design

text.content: Lug design can also influence how the tire under load compares to the industry standard when fully inflated.

rich_text.plain_text: Lug design can also influence how the tire under load compares to the industry standard when fully inflated.

text.content: Bead and sidewall optimization by each manufacturer can impact the type and width of the rim recommended that could deviate from the industry standard as an alternative rim fitment.

rich_text.plain_text: Bead and sidewall optimization by each manufacturer can impact the type and width of the rim recommended that could deviate from the industry standard as an alternative rim fitment.

text.content: Common Problems from Mismatched Tires

rich_text.plain_text: Common Problems from Mismatched Tires

text.content:
In agriculture, the application and operation most affects tire design and performance within the industry standards. As agricultural tires are considered “off-road” and not US Department of Transportation (DOT) regulated, there is some flexibility in the design process for each tire manufacturer to enhance features to deliver improved performance.

In all instances, the type of machinery, soil conditions, application, and air pressure maintenance will have a direct impact on its performance even after improvements. Here’s some things to keep in mind when using tires from different manufacturers:

text.content: To get the best from your tires, understand that these small changes in tire manufacturers can affect the performance of your farm equipment. When you match up your Ag tires properly, you reduce the chances of tire slip, improve fuel efficiency, reduce tire wear, and minimize soil compaction. Check out our website to learn more about our lineup of radial agriculture tires or jump into some more learning on
